question: What is Novo Nordisk's technical/options/quant setup?

findings.claim: NVO is trading massively below both key moving averages: 50-day SMA at $47.90 (price 23.1% below) and 200-day SMA at $54.73 (price 32.7% below), confirming a strong downtrend
findings.data: Computed from 251 daily bars (2025-03-24 to 2026-03-23). Latest close: $36.84. 50-day SMA: $47.90. 200-day SMA: $54.73. Death cross has been in effect since at least 50 days ago (2026-01-09: 50SMA=$49.49 vs 200SMA=$59.34).

findings.claim: 52-week range is $35.85 to $81.41 — current price is only 2.7% above the 52-week low ($35.85 on 2026-03-03), and 54.8% below the 52-week high
findings.data: 52-week high: $81.41 (2025-06-13). 52-week low: $35.85 (2026-03-03). Current: $36.84 (2026-03-23). Note: the all-time ADR high was ~$147 in mid-2024, making this a 75% drawdown from peak.

findings.claim: RSI(14) is at 32.1, approaching oversold territory (below 30) but not yet there, suggesting the selling is persistent but not at full capitulation
findings.data: RSI(14) computed via Wilder's smoothing method from 251 daily closes: 32.1. Neutral RSI is 50; below 30 is considered oversold. The stock has been grinding lower rather than flash-crashing in recent days.
